sourceMovie.title: Oldboy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Oldboy for the same reason it broke through in 2003: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Park Chan-wook keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Get Out
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Oldboy clicked for you, Get Out (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Sin City: A Dame to Kill For
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Oldboy clicked for you, Sin City: A Dame to Kill For (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Oldboy?
faq.answer: Try Get Out, Sin City: A Dame to Kill For, Out of the Furnace—each shares DNA with Oldboy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Oldboy?
faq.answer: Oldboy (2003) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Mystery, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Oldboy worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
